title: CPR

steps.title: Check the scene & the person
steps.instruction: Make sure the scene is safe. Tap the person's shoulder and shout 'Are you OK?' If there is no response, no breathing, or only gasping — act immediately.

steps.title: Call for help
steps.instruction: Call 101 immediately or tell someone nearby to call while you begin CPR. If alone, call first then start CPR. Get an AED if one is available nearby.

steps.title: Position the person
steps.instruction: Place the person on their back on a firm, flat surface. Kneel beside them. Make sure their arms are at their sides.

steps.title: Hand position for compressions
steps.instruction: Place the lower palm of one hand on the center of the chest, between the nipples. Place your other hand on top. Keep elbows straight and shoulders directly above your hands.

steps.title: Give 30 chest compressions
steps.instruction: Push hard and fast — at least 2 inches (5 cm) deep. Do 100 to 120 compressions per minute. Think of the beat of 'Stayin' Alive'. Allow the chest to fully rise between each compression. Count aloud: 1, 2, 3... 30.

steps.title: Open the airway
steps.instruction: If trained in CPR: tilt the head back gently with one hand on the forehead. With the other hand, lift the chin forward to open the airway.

steps.title: Give 2 rescue breaths
steps.instruction: Pinch the nose shut. Make a complete seal over their mouth with yours. Give 1 breath lasting 1 second — watch for the chest to rise. Give a second breath. If chest does not rise, retilt the head and try again. If not trained, skip breaths and continue compressions only.

steps.title: Continue cycles
steps.instruction: Continue cycles of 30 compressions and 2 breaths. Do not stop for more than 10 seconds. Keep going until the person shows signs of life, an AED is ready to use, or emergency services take over.

steps.title: Use AED if available
steps.instruction: As soon as an AED is available, turn it on and follow its voice instructions. Give one shock if advised, then immediately resume CPR for 2 more minutes before the next shock.

warnings: Do NOT stop compressions for more than 10 seconds
Do NOT compress less than 2 inches deep
Do NOT give too many breaths or breathe with too much force
Do NOT delay CPR — every second without blood flow causes brain damage
